functions.name: Govern
functions.description: Establish and monitor the organization's cybersecurity risk management strategy, expectations, and policy to support the organization's mission and stakeholder needs.

categories.name: Organizational Context
categories.description: The circumstances — mission, stakeholder expectations, legal and regulatory obligations, and critical services — that inform cybersecurity risk management.

subcategories.name: The organizational mission is understood and informs cybersecurity risk management
subcategories.description: The organization's mission, vision, and strategic objectives are formally documented and used as a foundation for establishing cybersecurity priorities and risk tolerance.
implementation_examples: Conduct annual workshops with executive leadership to document and review the organizational mission statement and strategic objectives
Map cybersecurity program goals directly to mission-critical functions and services in the risk register
Include mission impact assessments in all cybersecurity risk assessment templates to ensure alignment with organizational priorities

subcategories.name: Internal and external stakeholders are understood and their needs and expectations inform cybersecurity risk management
subcategories.description: Stakeholder identification and engagement processes ensure that the perspectives of customers, partners, regulators, employees, and other parties are incorporated into cybersecurity strategy.
implementation_examples: Maintain a stakeholder registry that maps each stakeholder group to their cybersecurity-relevant needs and communication preferences
Conduct quarterly stakeholder feedback sessions to capture evolving expectations regarding data protection and service availability
Integrate stakeholder requirements into the organization's cybersecurity risk appetite statement and control selection processes

subcategories.name: Legal, regulatory, and contractual obligations regarding cybersecurity are understood and managed
subcategories.description: The organization identifies and tracks applicable laws, regulations, and contractual commitments related to cybersecurity and incorporates them into policy and control frameworks.
implementation_examples: Maintain a legal obligations register with cross-references to applicable controls for GDPR, HIPAA, PCI-DSS, SOX, and sector-specific regulations
Engage legal counsel to review new contracts and regulatory changes for cybersecurity implications quarterly
Automate compliance mapping using a governance platform that links regulatory requirements to specific policy statements and technical controls

subcategories.name: Critical objectives, capabilities, and services are identified and inform cybersecurity risk management
subcategories.description: The organization identifies which services, processes, and capabilities are most critical to its mission and prioritizes cybersecurity investments accordingly.
implementation_examples: Conduct a business impact analysis (BIA) annually to identify critical services and their supporting assets
Develop a critical service dependency map showing how people, technology, and facilities support each essential business function
Prioritize cybersecurity resource allocation and control deployment based on critical service tiers defined in the BIA

categories.name: Risk Management Strategy
categories.description: The organization's priorities, constraints, risk tolerances, and assumptions are established and used to support operational risk decisions.

subcategories.name: Risk management objectives are established and agreed to by organizational stakeholders
subcategories.description: Clear, documented risk management goals are developed with input from leadership, business units, and technical teams to guide cybersecurity activities.
implementation_examples: Facilitate cross-functional workshops to define and document risk management objectives that align with enterprise strategy
Publish a risk management charter signed by executive leadership that states objectives, scope, and success criteria
Review risk management objectives annually during strategic planning cycles and adjust based on threat landscape changes

subcategories.name: Risk appetite and risk tolerance statements are established, communicated, and maintained
subcategories.description: Formal statements defining the amount and type of risk the organization is willing to accept are documented and regularly reviewed.
implementation_examples: Develop a risk appetite statement approved by the board of directors that quantifies acceptable risk levels across different risk categories
Create risk tolerance thresholds for key cybersecurity metrics such as mean time to detect (MTTD) and patch latency
Communicate risk appetite and tolerance statements through policy documents, training, and performance dashboards

subcategories.name: Cybersecurity risk management activities are aligned with enterprise risk management
subcategories.description: Cybersecurity risk is managed as an integral component of the organization's enterprise risk management (ERM) program rather than as a standalone function.
implementation_examples: Integrate cybersecurity risk register into the enterprise risk management platform for unified reporting and analysis
Assign cybersecurity risk owners from the ERM framework and include cyber risks in enterprise risk heat maps
Present cybersecurity risk posture alongside financial, operational, and compliance risks at quarterly board risk briefings

categories.name: Roles, Responsibilities, and Authorities
categories.description: Cybersecurity roles, responsibilities, and authorities are defined and communicated to foster accountability and effective governance.

subcategories.name: Cybersecurity roles and responsibilities are defined and communicated throughout the organization
subcategories.description: Clear role definitions for cybersecurity functions are established, documented, and communicated to all personnel.
implementation_examples: Publish a cybersecurity RACI matrix that assigns responsibility, accountability, consulted, and informed roles for each key process
Include cybersecurity role descriptions in all relevant job postings and position descriptions
Conduct an annual review of the cybersecurity organizational structure and role definitions to ensure alignment with current needs

subcategories.name: Cybersecurity roles and responsibilities are coordinated and aligned with internal and external stakeholders
subcategories.description: Roles and accountabilities are harmonized across internal teams and with external partners, suppliers, and regulators.
implementation_examples: Establish a cross-functional cybersecurity steering committee with representatives from IT, legal, HR, finance, and operations
Define and document cybersecurity liaison roles for each business unit to facilitate communication with the security team
Include cybersecurity role coordination requirements in contracts with managed security service providers and strategic partners

subcategories.name: Authority and accountability for cybersecurity risk management are defined and communicated
subcategories.description: Decision-making authority for cybersecurity risk acceptance, exception handling, and resource allocation is clearly assigned and communicated.
implementation_examples: Document and publish a cybersecurity authority matrix specifying who can accept risks, approve exceptions, and authorize security expenditures
Establish a risk acceptance process that requires documented sign-off at the appropriate leadership level based on risk severity
Include authority and accountability definitions in the cybersecurity governance charter and board-approved policies

categories.name: Policy
categories.description: Cybersecurity policies are established, communicated, and enforced to guide behavior and decision-making across the organization.

subcategories.name: Cybersecurity policy is established, communicated, and enforced
subcategories.description: A comprehensive cybersecurity policy framework is developed, approved by leadership, and actively enforced through technical and administrative controls.
implementation_examples: Develop a hierarchical policy framework with an overarching cybersecurity policy supported by standards, procedures, and guidelines
Require all employees and contractors to acknowledge the cybersecurity policy annually through the learning management system
Enforce policy compliance through technical controls such as automated configuration checks, network access controls, and endpoint detection

subcategories.name: Cybersecurity policy is reviewed, updated, and communicated on a regular and event-driven basis
subcategories.description: Policies are maintained through periodic reviews and are updated in response to changes in the threat landscape, regulatory requirements, or business operations.
implementation_examples: Schedule annual policy review cycles with a documented change log and version history for each policy document
Trigger policy updates upon major regulatory changes, significant security incidents, or organizational restructuring
Use a policy management platform to automate review reminders, track approvals, and notify stakeholders of updated policies

subcategories.name: Cybersecurity policy exceptions are managed through a formal process
subcategories.description: A structured exception management process ensures that deviations from policy are documented, risk-assessed, approved, and tracked for remediation.
implementation_examples: Establish an exception request form that requires business justification, risk assessment, compensating controls, and expiration date
Implement a policy exception tracking dashboard that notifies owners of approaching expiration dates
Review all open exceptions quarterly at the cybersecurity steering committee and escalate recurring exceptions for resolution

categories.name: Oversight
categories.description: Cybersecurity risk management activities and outcomes are reviewed to ensure alignment with organizational objectives and continuous improvement.

subcategories.name: Cybersecurity risk management strategy is reviewed and updated
subcategories.description: Leadership reviews the cybersecurity risk management strategy periodically to ensure it remains relevant, effective, and aligned with organizational objectives.
implementation_examples: Conduct an annual cybersecurity strategy review with the board of directors or equivalent governing body
Benchmark the cybersecurity strategy against industry peers and recognized frameworks such as NIST CSF 2.0
Update the cybersecurity strategy document with lessons learned from incidents, audit findings, and changes in the threat landscape

subcategories.name: The cybersecurity program is reviewed and results inform organizational decisions
subcategories.description: The effectiveness of the cybersecurity program is evaluated through metrics, audits, and assessments, and findings are used to guide decision-making.
implementation_examples: Develop a cybersecurity dashboard with key performance indicators (KPIs) and key risk indicators (KRIs) for executive reporting
Conduct biennial internal and external audits of the cybersecurity program against established frameworks
Present cybersecurity program performance and improvement recommendations to leadership on a quarterly basis

subcategories.name: Cybersecurity risk management is included in the organization's broader enterprise risk management activities
subcategories.description: Cybersecurity is treated as a component of enterprise risk management, with cyber risks consolidated into the organization-wide risk profile.
implementation_examples: Ensure the Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) reports to the enterprise risk management committee or equivalent body
Aggregate cybersecurity risk data into the enterprise risk register using a standardized risk taxonomy
Align cybersecurity risk scenario analysis with enterprise risk scenario planning and stress testing exercises

categories.name: Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management
categories.description: Cybersecurity supply chain risk management practices are integrated into the organization's overall risk management to address risks from third-party products and services.

subcategories.name: A cybersecurity supply chain risk management program is established and integrated into the organization's risk management program
subcategories.description: A formal program is created to identify, assess, and manage cybersecurity risks associated with the organization's supply chain.
implementation_examples: Develop a supply chain cybersecurity policy that defines program scope, roles, and minimum security requirements for all suppliers
Integrate supply chain risk assessment into the enterprise risk management framework with dedicated risk categories
Assign a supply chain risk manager responsible for maintaining the program and coordinating with procurement and legal teams

subcategories.name: Cybersecurity requirements are established for suppliers
subcategories.description: The organization defines and documents baseline cybersecurity requirements that all suppliers must meet before, during, and after the engagement.
implementation_examples: Create a supplier cybersecurity requirements checklist aligned with industry standards such as NIST SP 800-171 or ISO 27001
Include data protection, incident notification, and right-to-audit clauses in all supplier contracts
Define tiered security requirements based on supplier data access levels and criticality to organizational operations

subcategories.name: Cybersecurity requirements are integrated into procurement processes and contracts
subcategories.description: Cybersecurity considerations are embedded in the procurement lifecycle from vendor selection through contract management and termination.
implementation_examples: Require cybersecurity review as a mandatory step in the procurement approval workflow for all technology-related purchases
Include cybersecurity service level agreements (SLAs) and breach notification timelines in contracts with all critical suppliers
Conduct security assessments of prospective suppliers during the due diligence phase before contract award

subcategories.name: Suppliers are assessed to ensure cybersecurity requirements are met
subcategories.description: Ongoing assessment of supplier cybersecurity posture is conducted through questionnaires, audits, and continuous monitoring.
implementation_examples: Implement a tiered supplier assessment program with annual self-assessments for low-risk and on-site audits for high-risk suppliers
Use a third-party risk management platform to automate questionnaire distribution, evidence collection, and risk scoring
Monitor supplier security posture continuously through external attack surface scanning and breach notification monitoring services

subcategories.name: Response and recovery planning for supply chain incidents is established
subcategories.description: Plans are developed to respond to and recover from cybersecurity incidents that originate from or affect third-party suppliers and service providers.
implementation_examples: Include supply chain incident scenarios in the organization's incident response playbook with specific containment and communication procedures
Develop contingency plans for critical supplier outages, including identification of alternative suppliers and manual workaround procedures
Conduct tabletop exercises annually that simulate supply chain compromise scenarios involving key vendors and partners

functions.name: Identify
functions.description: Develop organizational understanding of cybersecurity risks to systems, people, assets, data, and capabilities.

categories.name: Asset Management
categories.description: Assets, including hardware, software, data, and external systems, are identified and managed consistent with their criticality and risk.

subcategories.name: Inventories of hardware managed by the organization are maintained
subcategories.description: A complete and accurate inventory of all hardware assets is maintained and kept current to support risk management and incident response activities.
implementation_examples: Deploy an agent-based asset discovery tool that continuously scans the network and reports new or modified hardware to a central CMDB
Conduct quarterly physical asset audits in data centers and critical facility areas to verify inventory accuracy
Integrate hardware inventory data with the configuration management database and incident response systems for automated correlation

subcategories.name: Inventories of software, services, and systems managed by the organization are maintained
subcategories.description: Software assets including operating systems, applications, and cloud services are inventoried and tracked throughout their lifecycle.
implementation_examples: Implement a software asset management tool that automatically discovers installed applications and tracks license compliance
Maintain a service catalog that documents all internal and external services, their owners, and their data classifications
Integrate software inventory with vulnerability management systems to identify and prioritize patching activities

subcategories.name: Data is managed consistent with the organization's risk strategy
subcategories.description: Data classification, handling, and retention practices are aligned with the organization's risk appetite and regulatory requirements.
implementation_examples: Implement a data classification policy with labels such as public, internal, confidential, and restricted, and enforce handling rules through DLP controls
Deploy automated data discovery and classification tools that scan structured and unstructured data repositories
Establish data retention schedules and automated purging processes to minimize data exposure risk

subcategories.name: Inventories of external information systems are maintained
subcategories.description: External systems and services used by the organization, including cloud platforms, SaaS applications, and partner interfaces, are identified and tracked.
implementation_examples: Maintain a shadow IT discovery program that uses network logs and SaaS access data to identify unapproved external services
Create a cloud services registry that documents each service, its data handling practices, and its security certification status
Review and update the external system inventory quarterly through collaboration with procurement and business unit stakeholders

subcategories.name: Assets are prioritized based on classification, criticality, and business value
subcategories.description: Assets are assigned priority levels that reflect their importance to mission delivery, enabling risk-based allocation of security resources.
implementation_examples: Develop a criticality scoring methodology that considers data sensitivity, regulatory impact, and mission dependency for each asset
Tag assets in the CMDB with criticality tiers (Tier 1 through Tier 3) and apply differentiated security controls per tier
Ensure high-criticality assets receive priority attention in vulnerability management, monitoring, and incident response processes

categories.name: Risk Assessment
categories.description: Cybersecurity risk is identified, analyzed, and communicated to inform decision-making and prioritization.

subcategories.name: Vulnerabilities in assets are identified and validated
subcategories.description: Systematic vulnerability identification and validation processes ensure that weaknesses are discovered and confirmed before threat actors can exploit them.
implementation_examples: Run authenticated vulnerability scans against all internal assets on a weekly schedule and external assets continuously
Establish a vulnerability validation workflow that confirms findings through manual testing before generating remediation tickets
Integrate threat intelligence feeds with vulnerability scan results to prioritize exploitation-prone vulnerabilities

subcategories.name: Threat intelligence is received and analyzed
subcategories.description: The organization collects and analyzes threat intelligence from multiple sources to understand the current threat landscape and inform defensive priorities.
implementation_examples: Subscribe to sector-specific Information Sharing and Analysis Centers (ISACs) and integrate their feeds into the SIEM platform
Assign a threat intelligence analyst to curate, contextualize, and disseminate intelligence reports to relevant teams
Establish a threat intelligence platform that automates indicator ingestion and correlation with organizational asset data

subcategories.name: Threats, vulnerabilities, likelihoods, and impacts are used to determine risk
subcategories.description: Risk is assessed by analyzing the combination of threats, vulnerabilities, likelihood of occurrence, and potential business impact.
implementation_examples: Adopt a quantitative risk analysis methodology such as FAIR to model cyber risk scenarios in financial terms
Conduct annual risk assessments that evaluate threats against the asset inventory using a standardized risk scoring matrix
Maintain a risk register that captures threat-vulnerability pairs with likelihood, impact, and inherent risk scores

subcategories.name: Risk responses are identified and prioritized
subcategories.description: Organizational risk responses — accept, avoid, mitigate, transfer, or escalate — are formally identified and prioritized based on residual risk levels.
implementation_examples: Establish a risk treatment process that documents the selected response option, responsible party, and target completion date for each risk
Prioritize risk remediation based on residual risk scores and alignment with organizational risk appetite thresholds
Maintain a risk treatment plan that tracks mitigation progress and escalates overdue items to leadership

subcategories.name: Risk is communicated to stakeholders
subcategories.description: Risk assessment results and treatment status are communicated to relevant stakeholders in a timely and understandable manner.
implementation_examples: Publish a quarterly cybersecurity risk report for leadership that summarizes top risks, treatment progress, and emerging concerns
Develop role-specific risk briefings that translate technical risk findings into business impact language for non-technical stakeholders
Establish a risk communication protocol that defines escalation thresholds, notification timelines, and distribution lists

subcategories.name: Risk is tracked and monitored over time
subcategories.description: Risks are continuously monitored and re-evaluated to reflect changes in the threat landscape, business environment, or control effectiveness.
implementation_examples: Implement a risk management dashboard that provides real-time visibility into risk scores, treatment status, and trend analysis
Schedule periodic risk review meetings to reassess existing risks and identify new ones based on recent events or intelligence
Automate risk scoring updates by feeding vulnerability scan data, threat intelligence, and control effectiveness metrics into the risk register

subcategories.name: Risk scenarios are developed to inform strategic planning and resource allocation
subcategories.description: The organization develops and maintains a set of risk scenarios that model potential cyber events and their business impact to guide investment decisions.
implementation_examples: Develop a library of risk scenarios covering ransomware, data breach, supply chain compromise, and cloud service outage
Use scenario analysis results to inform cybersecurity budget planning and insurance coverage decisions
Update risk scenarios annually based on lessons learned from actual incidents, industry trends, and emerging threat actor tactics

categories.name: Improvement
categories.description: Improvements to the cybersecurity program are identified and integrated based on lessons learned, assessments, and changes in the operating environment.

subcategories.name: Improvements are identified and integrated into the cybersecurity program
subcategories.description: Findings from assessments, audits, incidents, and exercises are systematically captured and converted into actionable improvement initiatives.
implementation_examples: Maintain a cybersecurity improvement register that tracks all identified enhancements, their priority, owner, and status
Conduct post-incident reviews within 30 days of major incidents and track resulting improvement actions to closure
Incorporate improvement recommendations from risk assessments, penetration tests, and audits into the next planning cycle

subcategories.name: The cybersecurity program is reviewed and updated to address changing requirements
subcategories.description: The cybersecurity program undergoes regular review cycles to ensure it remains effective and aligned with evolving business needs and threat conditions.
implementation_examples: Conduct an annual program effectiveness review using a maturity model such as the NIST CSF 2.0 implementation tiers
Track program metrics against industry benchmarks and adjust resources and priorities based on gap analysis results
Engage an independent third party to perform a biennial cybersecurity program assessment against recognized standards

subcategories.name: Lessons learned from incidents and exercises are incorporated
subcategories.description: Knowledge gained from actual incidents and simulated exercises is captured and used to improve processes, controls, and response capabilities.
implementation_examples: Establish a formal lessons-learned process that includes facilitated debriefs, root cause analysis, and documented action items
Update incident response playbooks, detection rules, and security controls based on lessons learned within 60 days of each significant incident
Share anonymized lessons learned across the organization through bulletins, training updates, and process improvements

categories.name: Supply Chain Risk Management
categories.description: Supply chain risk management practices are established to identify, assess, and respond to risks from third-party products and services.

subcategories.name: A supply chain risk management program is established and integrated into the organization's risk management program
subcategories.description: A formal program to manage cybersecurity risks from the supply chain is defined, resourced, and aligned with enterprise risk management.
implementation_examples: Develop and publish a supply chain risk management policy approved by executive leadership and aligned with the enterprise risk framework
Establish a cross-functional supply chain risk working group with representatives from procurement, legal, IT, and security
Integrate supply chain risk management into the organization's overall risk management methodology and reporting structure

subcategories.name: Cybersecurity requirements are established for suppliers
subcategories.description: Baseline cybersecurity requirements are defined and documented for all suppliers based on the criticality of products and services provided.
implementation_examples: Create a supplier cybersecurity requirements matrix that maps security controls to supplier risk tiers
Reference established standards such as NIST SP 800-171, ISO 27001, or SOC 2 as baseline requirements in supplier agreements
Define minimum cybersecurity requirements for data protection, access control, incident response, and business continuity in all contracts

subcategories.name: Cybersecurity requirements are integrated into procurement processes and contracts
subcategories.description: Cybersecurity considerations are incorporated throughout the procurement lifecycle, from vendor selection to contract management and termination.
implementation_examples: Add cybersecurity review as a mandatory approval gate in the procurement workflow for all technology and data-related purchases
Include right-to-audit clauses, breach notification requirements, and cybersecurity SLAs in all supplier contracts
Develop contract termination procedures that ensure secure data return or destruction when supplier relationships end

subcategories.name: Suppliers are assessed to ensure cybersecurity requirements are met
subcategories.description: Ongoing assessment of supplier cybersecurity posture is conducted using a risk-based approach that scales with supplier criticality.
implementation_examples: Implement a supplier risk scoring methodology that incorporates questionnaire responses, external ratings, and security scan results
Conduct on-site audits for high-risk suppliers annually and review their security certifications and penetration test reports
Use a third-party risk management platform to automate evidence collection, remediation tracking, and reporting

subcategories.name: Response and recovery planning for supply chain incidents is established
subcategories.description: Plans are developed to respond to and recover from cybersecurity incidents involving third-party suppliers and their products or services.
implementation_examples: Develop supply chain incident response playbooks that address scenarios such as supplier data breach, service outage, and software backdoor discovery
Include critical suppliers in business continuity and disaster recovery testing and exercises
Maintain a list of alternative suppliers for critical products and services to support rapid recovery from supply chain disruptions

functions.name: Protect
functions.description: Safeguards are deployed to manage and reduce cybersecurity risk to organizational assets, data, and capabilities.

categories.name: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control
categories.description: Identities and access for users, devices, and services are managed to protect organizational assets.

subcategories.name: Identities and credentials are issued, managed, and verified
subcategories.description: The organization manages the full lifecycle of identities and credentials, including issuance, maintenance, and revocation.
implementation_examples: Implement a centralized identity management platform that provisions and de-provisions accounts automatically based on HR system triggers
Enforce password complexity requirements and multi-factor authentication for all user accounts including administrative and privileged users
Conduct quarterly identity reconciliation reviews to detect and remove orphaned or unauthorized accounts

subcategories.name: Physical access to assets is managed
subcategories.description: Physical access to facilities, data centers, and sensitive equipment is controlled, monitored, and audited.
implementation_examples: Deploy badge-based access control systems with role-based zone access restrictions in all data centers and server rooms
Maintain a visitor management process that requires escorts, sign-in, and restricted access for all non-employees
Conduct quarterly audits of physical access logs and reconcile them against authorized personnel lists

subcategories.name: Remote access is managed
subcategories.description: Remote access to organizational systems and data is secured through authentication, authorization, and encryption controls.
implementation_examples: Require VPN connections with device posture checks and multi-factor authentication for all remote access to internal systems
Implement just-in-time and just-enough-access policies for remote administrative sessions
Maintain a remote access policy that specifies approved methods, security requirements, and periodic recertification

subcategories.name: Access permissions are managed and enforced
subcategories.description: Access permissions are granted based on the principle of least privilege and are regularly reviewed and recertified.
implementation_examples: Implement role-based access control (RBAC) across all major systems and applications with clearly defined role definitions
Conduct quarterly access recertification campaigns where managers review and approve or revoke their team members' access
Deploy a privileged access management (PAM) solution to manage, monitor, and audit elevated access to critical systems

subcategories.name: Network integrity is protected
subcategories.description: Network boundaries and internal segments are secured to protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data in transit.
implementation_examples: Implement network segmentation with firewalls separating production, development, and corporate networks
Deploy network access control (NAC) solutions to enforce endpoint compliance before granting network access
Monitor network traffic for unauthorized connections and anomalous patterns using network detection and response tools

subcategories.name: Least privilege and separation of duties are enforced
subcategories.description: Users and systems are granted only the minimum privileges necessary to perform their functions, and conflicting duties are segregated.
implementation_examples: Map and enforce separation of duties matrices for financial systems, change management, and administrative functions
Implement privileged access workflows that require approval and logging for all elevated privilege escalations
Conduct biannual reviews of privilege assignments to identify and remove excessive permissions or conflicting role combinations

subcategories.name: Access for non-human entities such as service accounts and APIs is managed and monitored
subcategories.description: Service accounts, API tokens, machine identities, and other non-human access credentials are securely managed and monitored for misuse.
implementation_examples: Implement a secrets management platform to securely store, rotate, and audit service account credentials and API tokens
Monitor service account activity for anomalous behavior such as unusual login times, locations, or data access patterns
Require periodic rotation of service account credentials and API keys with automated enforcement through the secrets management platform

categories.name: Awareness and Training
categories.description: Personnel are provided cybersecurity awareness and training to support a security-conscious culture.

subcategories.name: Awareness and training needs are identified
subcategories.description: The organization assesses and documents the cybersecurity awareness and training needs for all personnel based on their roles and responsibilities.
implementation_examples: Conduct an annual training needs analysis that maps job functions to required cybersecurity competencies
Develop role-based training paths for general users, power users, developers, administrators, and executives
Use phishing simulation results and incident trends to identify targeted training needs for specific teams or departments

subcategories.name: Personnel are trained to fulfill their cybersecurity roles and responsibilities
subcategories.description: All personnel receive appropriate cybersecurity training to understand and fulfill their assigned roles and responsibilities.
implementation_examples: Deliver annual cybersecurity awareness training to all employees with role-specific modules for privileged users
Include cybersecurity training in new hire onboarding with mandatory completion before system access is granted
Track training completion rates and provide remediation training for personnel who fail phishing simulations or security assessments

subcategories.name: Personnel are trained to recognize and report cybersecurity incidents
subcategories.description: Personnel are equipped with the knowledge to identify potential security incidents and understand the proper channels and procedures for reporting them.
implementation_examples: Conduct regular phishing simulation campaigns with immediate in-the-moment training for users who click on simulated phishing links
Publish and communicate clear incident reporting procedures including a 24/7 security hotline and email reporting address
Provide training on how to recognize social engineering techniques, suspicious attachments, and unusual system behaviors

categories.name: Data Security
categories.description: Data is managed consistently with the organization's risk strategy to protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information.

subcategories.name: Data-at-rest is protected
subcategories.description: Data stored on systems, devices, and media is protected through encryption, access controls, and physical security measures.
implementation_examples: Encrypt all data at rest on servers, databases, and endpoints using AES-256 or equivalent strong encryption algorithms
Implement full-disk encryption on all laptops, mobile devices, and removable media with centralized key management
Apply database-level encryption for sensitive data fields and enforce row-level security for multi-tenant data stores

subcategories.name: Data-in-transit is protected
subcategories.description: Data transmitted across networks is protected through encryption and integrity controls to prevent interception or tampering.
implementation_examples: Enforce TLS 1.2 or higher for all web traffic, email communications, and API data transmissions
Implement VPN encryption for all remote access traffic and inter-datacenter communications
Configure network devices to enforce encryption protocols and disable unencrypted protocols such as Telnet, FTP, and HTTP where possible

subcategories.name: Assets are formally managed throughout removal, transfers, and disposition
subcategories.description: Formal processes govern the decommissioning, transfer, or disposal of assets to ensure data is securely erased and assets are properly accounted for.
implementation_examples: Implement a hardware decommissioning process that includes secure data wiping using NIST SP 800-88 standards
Maintain an asset disposal log that documents serial numbers, destruction methods, and certificates of destruction
Establish a transfer procedure that requires data encryption and inventory verification before assets are moved between locations

subcategories.name: Adequate capacity to ensure availability is maintained
subcategories.description: System capacity is monitored and managed to ensure that availability requirements are met under normal and peak conditions.
implementation_examples: Monitor storage, compute, and network capacity with automated alerting when utilization exceeds 80% of capacity thresholds
Conduct annual capacity planning reviews that project growth requirements and budget for necessary expansions
Implement auto-scaling capabilities for cloud workloads to handle demand spikes without manual intervention

subcategories.name: Protections against data leaks are implemented
subcategories.description: Technical and administrative controls are deployed to detect and prevent unauthorized data exfiltration or leakage.
implementation_examples: Deploy data loss prevention (DLP) solutions that monitor and block unauthorized transfers of sensitive data across email, web, and endpoints
Implement data classification labels and automated labeling policies in document management and collaboration platforms
Configure cloud access security brokers (CASB) to monitor and control data sharing activities in SaaS applications

subcategories.name: Integrity checking mechanisms are used to verify software, firmware, and information integrity
subcategories.description: Mechanisms such as checksums, digital signatures, and file integrity monitoring are employed to detect unauthorized modifications.
implementation_examples: Deploy file integrity monitoring (FIM) agents on critical servers to detect unauthorized changes to system files and configurations
Validate software downloads and updates by verifying cryptographic hashes and digital signatures before installation
Implement code signing for all internally developed software and enforce integrity verification in the CI/CD pipeline

subcategories.name: The development and testing environment(s) are separate from the production environment
subcategories.description: Development, testing, and production environments are logically or physically separated to prevent accidental exposure or modification of production data and systems.
implementation_examples: Maintain separate network segments and infrastructure accounts for development, staging, and production environments
Implement strict change management processes that require code promotion through defined environments before reaching production
Prohibit the use of production data in non-production environments or enforce data masking for any copies used in development testing

subcategories.name: Data backup and recovery procedures are aligned with business continuity and disaster recovery requirements
subcategories.description: Data backup strategies are designed to meet recovery point objectives (RPO) and recovery time objectives (RTO) defined in business continuity plans.
implementation_examples: Implement the 3-2-1 backup strategy with at least three copies, two different media types, and one off-site copy
Test backup restoration procedures quarterly and document recovery times against stated RTO targets
Encrypt all backup data at rest and in transit and store backups in geographically diverse locations to prevent simultaneous loss

categories.name: Platform Security
categories.description: The organization's platforms, including hardware, software, and infrastructure, are configured and maintained to reduce cybersecurity risk.

subcategories.name: Configuration management practices are established and applied
subcategories.description: Secure baseline configurations are defined and enforced for all technology platforms to reduce vulnerabilities and maintain consistency.
implementation_examples: Develop and maintain security configuration baselines for operating systems, network devices, and applications aligned with CIS Benchmarks
Deploy configuration management tools to automatically enforce baseline configurations and detect drift across the infrastructure
Include configuration review checkpoints in the change management process for all new system deployments and major updates

subcategories.name: Software is maintained, replaced, and removed in accordance with organizational policies
subcategories.description: Software lifecycle management ensures that only authorized, supported, and patched software is deployed, and unsupported software is removed.
implementation_examples: Maintain an approved software catalog and block unauthorized software installations through application allowlisting or execution controls
Establish a patch management process that applies security patches within defined SLAs based on vulnerability severity
Identify and remove unsupported or end-of-life software through quarterly reconciliation of the software inventory against vendor lifecycle dates

subcategories.name: Maintenance and repair of assets is performed and logged
subcategories.description: Maintenance and repair activities for organizational assets are authorized, performed securely, and logged for audit purposes.
implementation_examples: Establish a maintenance schedule for critical infrastructure equipment and track all activities in a maintenance management system
Require vendor service personnel to sign non-disclosure agreements and be escorted during on-site maintenance activities
Implement a maintenance log that records the technician, date, scope of work, and replaced components for each maintenance activity

subcategories.name: Log records are generated and made available for monitoring and analysis
subcategories.description: Logging is enabled across systems and applications to generate records that support security monitoring, incident response, and forensics.
implementation_examples: Configure centralized log collection from all critical systems including servers, network devices, security appliances, and cloud platforms
Define a log retention policy that meets regulatory requirements and incident investigation needs, with minimum 12-month retention
Implement a security information and event management (SIEM) platform to correlate log data and generate actionable alerts

subcategories.name: Secure software development practices are integrated throughout the development lifecycle
subcategories.description: Security is embedded into each phase of the software development lifecycle to minimize vulnerabilities before deployment.
implementation_examples: Integrate static application security testing (SAST) and dynamic application security testing (DAST) into the CI/CD pipeline
Conduct threat modeling sessions during the design phase of all new software development projects
Maintain a secure coding standards document and enforce code review requirements including security checklist items

subcategories.name: Vulnerability management practices are established
subcategories.description: A formal vulnerability management program identifies, classifies, prioritizes, and remediates vulnerabilities across the technology estate.
implementation_examples: Deploy vulnerability scanners for network, web application, cloud, and container environments with scheduled and on-demand scanning capabilities
Establish vulnerability remediation SLAs based on severity levels with critical vulnerabilities requiring remediation within 48 hours
Create a vulnerability management reporting dashboard that tracks mean time to remediate and vulnerability aging by severity

categories.name: Technology Infrastructure Resilience
categories.description: The organization's technology infrastructure is designed, deployed, and maintained to ensure resilience and continuity of operations.

subcategories.name: Backups of information and systems are managed and tested
subcategories.description: Data and system backups are performed regularly and tested to ensure they can be reliably restored in a timely manner.
implementation_examples: Automate daily backups of all critical systems and verify backup completion through monitoring and alerting
Conduct quarterly full restoration tests for critical systems and document recovery results against RPO and RTO targets
Maintain isolated or air-gapped backup copies to protect against ransomware encryption and destruction

subcategories.name: The network infrastructure is resilient
subcategories.description: Network architecture incorporates redundancy, failover, and capacity management to maintain connectivity during disruptions.
implementation_examples: Design network topology with redundant paths, diverse carrier connections, and automatic failover mechanisms
Implement software-defined networking capabilities to dynamically reroute traffic during network component failures
Conduct annual failover testing of network links and document switchover times and any degraded performance during tests

subcategories.name: Systems and services are resilient
subcategories.description: Systems are architected with redundancy and failover capabilities to maintain availability during component failures or cyber incidents.
implementation_examples: Deploy critical services in active-active or active-passive high-availability configurations across multiple availability zones
Implement load balancing and auto-scaling to distribute workloads and absorb traffic surges during denial of service events
Conduct semi-annual failover exercises for critical systems and validate that failover occurs within defined recovery time objectives

subcategories.name: Alternate processing capabilities are established
subcategories.description: Alternate processing sites or capabilities are established and maintained to support essential operations during extended disruptions.
implementation_examples: Establish a secondary data center or cloud region capable of supporting critical operations within RTO thresholds
Maintain standby infrastructure that can be activated during a major disaster or prolonged cyber attack
Conduct annual disaster recovery exercises that exercise full failover to alternate processing sites and validate restoration of all critical services

functions.name: Detect
functions.description: The organization identifies and analyzes potential cybersecurity adverse events to enable timely discovery and response.

categories.name: Adverse Event Analysis
categories.description: Adverse events are identified and analyzed to understand their nature, scope, and impact.

subcategories.name: Adverse events are collected and correlated
subcategories.description: Security event data from multiple sources is aggregated and correlated to identify patterns that may indicate adverse events.
implementation_examples: Configure the SIEM to collect and normalize logs from endpoints, network devices, cloud platforms, and identity providers
Implement correlation rules that link related events across different data sources to identify multi-stage attack sequences
Create composite alerts that fire only when multiple related events meet threshold criteria to reduce false positive noise

subcategories.name: Adverse events are analyzed to identify incidents
subcategories.description: Collected events are analyzed by automated systems and human analysts to distinguish malicious activity from benign events.
implementation_examples: Deploy a security operations center (SOC) tiered model where Tier 1 analysts triage alerts and escalate confirmed incidents to Tier 2
Implement user and entity behavior analytics (UEBA) to baseline normal activity and detect anomalous behavioral deviations
Use threat intelligence enrichment to automatically assess whether observed events correlate with known indicators of compromise

subcategories.name: Event data is aggregated and correlated from multiple sources
subcategories.description: Event information is gathered across disparate systems and sources to provide a unified view of the organization's security posture.
implementation_examples: Deploy centralized logging infrastructure that ingests data from on-premises systems, cloud services, and SaaS applications
Implement a data lake architecture for long-term event storage with efficient querying and analysis capabilities
Correlate identity and access logs with network and endpoint data to build complete timelines of user and system activity

subcategories.name: The impact of adverse events is determined
subcategories.description: Analysis processes assess the potential and actual impact of adverse events on organizational operations, assets, and stakeholders.
implementation_examples: Develop an impact assessment checklist that analysts use to evaluate the scope of affected systems, data types, and business functions
Integrate asset criticality data from the CMDB into the SIEM to automatically prioritize alerts involving high-value assets
Establish a process for real-time business impact assessment during active incidents with input from business unit liaisons

subcategories.name: Incident thresholds are established and applied
subcategories.description: Organizational thresholds defining what constitutes an incident versus a routine event are established and used to trigger response activities.
implementation_examples: Define incident classification criteria and severity levels with clear thresholds for declaring a formal incident
Automate incident declaration based on alert severity scores that consider asset criticality, threat confidence, and impact potential
Review and adjust incident thresholds quarterly based on false positive rates, changes in the environment, and lessons learned

categories.name: Continuous Monitoring
categories.description: The organization continuously monitors its assets, networks, and environment to detect cybersecurity threats and vulnerabilities.

subcategories.name: Networks and network services are monitored
subcategories.description: Network traffic and services are continuously monitored to detect suspicious activity, policy violations, and potential intrusions.
implementation_examples: Deploy network intrusion detection and prevention systems (IDS/IPS) at key network chokepoints and internet gateways
Implement network traffic analysis tools that use machine learning to identify anomalous traffic patterns and protocol violations
Monitor DNS, DHCP, and NetFlow data to detect command and control communications, data exfiltration, and network scanning activity

subcategories.name: The physical environment is monitored
subcategories.description: Physical security monitoring is in place to detect unauthorized physical access, tampering, or environmental hazards.
implementation_examples: Install video surveillance cameras with motion detection at all facility access points, data centers, and sensitive areas
Deploy environmental sensors that monitor temperature, humidity, smoke, and water leaks in data centers and server rooms
Implement physical access control systems with audit logging and real-time alerting for unauthorized access attempts

subcategories.name: Personnel activity is monitored
subcategories.description: User activity is monitored to detect insider threats, credential misuse, and policy violations that could indicate security incidents.
implementation_examples: Implement user activity monitoring solutions that track logon times, application usage, data access patterns, and file transfers
Monitor privileged user activity with session recording and keystroke logging for high-risk administrative accounts
Deploy insider threat detection analytics that correlate HR data with system activity to flag anomalous behavior

subcategories.name: Malicious code is detected
subcategories.description: Antivirus, anti-malware, and endpoint detection and response capabilities are deployed to identify and contain malicious code.
implementation_examples: Deploy endpoint detection and response (EDR) agents on all workstations, servers, and mobile devices with centralized management and alerting
Enable real-time malware scanning on email gateways, web proxies, and file servers to detect malicious code at multiple ingress points
Implement behavioral-based detection rules that identify malware-like activities such as process injection, persistence mechanisms, and ransomware patterns

subcategories.name: Unauthorized connections and unauthorized mobile code are detected
subcategories.description: The organization monitors for unauthorized network connections, rogue devices, and unauthorized mobile code execution.
implementation_examples: Deploy network access control (NAC) to detect and block unauthorized devices attempting to connect to the network
Monitor for unauthorized wireless access points and Bluetooth connections through periodic wireless site surveys and WIPS deployment
Configure application control policies to block execution of unauthorized scripts, macros, and mobile code in productivity applications

subcategories.name: Service provider activity is monitored
subcategories.description: Security-relevant activities of external service providers are monitored to detect incidents that could affect the organization.
implementation_examples: Monitor service provider access to internal systems through federated identity logs and privileged access session recording
Integrate service provider security notifications and breach alerts into the organization's incident management workflow
Conduct periodic reviews of service provider audit logs and security reports to identify anomalous access patterns or control failures

categories.name: Detection Processes
categories.description: Detection processes and procedures are maintained and tested to ensure timely and effective identification of cybersecurity events.

subcategories.name: Detection processes are established and managed
subcategories.description: Formal processes for detecting cybersecurity events are documented, resourced, and managed to ensure consistent operation.
implementation_examples: Develop and maintain a detection operations playbook that documents standard operating procedures for monitoring, triage, and escalation
Define and assign roles and responsibilities for detection activities including shift schedules for SOC analysts
Establish key performance indicators (KPIs) for detection such as mean time to detect, alert volume, and false positive rates

subcategories.name: Detection processes are tested and maintained
subcategories.description: Detection capabilities are regularly tested through validation exercises, penetration tests, and tabletop exercises to ensure effectiveness.
implementation_examples: Conduct red team exercises that simulate real-world attack scenarios to test the effectiveness of detection controls
Deploy breach and attack simulation (BAS) tools to continuously validate that security controls detect known attack techniques
Perform quarterly detection rule reviews to update alert logic based on new threats, false positive feedback, and changes in the environment

subcategories.name: Event data is correlated from multiple sources
subcategories.description: Data from diverse sources including network, endpoint, application, and threat intelligence feeds is correlated to identify complex attack patterns.
implementation_examples: Implement a security data lake that aggregates structured and unstructured security data from all monitoring sources for advanced analytics
Develop correlation rules that link authentication events with network connections and file access to detect lateral movement
Use threat intelligence enrichment to correlate internal events with known adversary infrastructure and tactics

subcategories.name: The impact of detection process failures is understood
subcategories.description: The organization understands the consequences of detection gaps or failures and addresses them through compensating controls and process improvements.
implementation_examples: Conduct gap analyses to identify attack techniques that current detection controls may miss and document the associated risk exposure
Implement compensating controls such as enhanced logging or manual review processes for areas where automated detection is limited
Perform a detection coverage assessment at least annually using frameworks such as MITRE ATT&CK to map detection capabilities

subcategories.name: Detection processes are continuously improved
subcategories.description: Detection capabilities are refined and enhanced through lessons learned, technology updates, and changes in the threat landscape.
implementation_examples: Hold regular retrospectives to review detection performance, identify improvement opportunities, and prioritize rule enhancements
Subscribe to threat intelligence feeds and industry detection content to stay current with emerging adversary tactics and techniques
Benchmark detection metrics against industry peers and implement improvement initiatives to close performance gaps

functions.name: Respond
functions.description: The organization takes action to contain, analyze, and mitigate cybersecurity incidents to minimize impact and restore normal operations.

categories.name: Incident Management
categories.description: Incidents are managed through a structured lifecycle from preparation and detection through containment, eradication, and recovery.

subcategories.name: The incident management plan is established and communicated
subcategories.description: A comprehensive incident management plan is developed, documented, and communicated to ensure all stakeholders understand their roles and responsibilities.
implementation_examples: Develop a formal incident response plan that includes roles, escalation paths, communication templates, and step-by-step procedures
Publish the incident response plan through the organization's policy management system and ensure all team members have offline access
Conduct an annual tabletop exercise to validate the plan and identify areas for improvement

subcategories.name: The incident management plan is executed
subcategories.description: The incident management plan is activated and executed in a timely manner when an incident is declared, following established procedures.
implementation_examples: Establish a 24/7 incident response hotline and on-call rotation that ensures a qualified responder is always available
Use incident management software to track all incidents through a structured lifecycle with defined stages, tasks, and timestamps
Conduct post-incident reviews within 30 days of each major incident to evaluate execution against the plan

subcategories.name: Coordination with stakeholders is established
subcategories.description: The organization coordinates incident response activities with internal and external stakeholders including legal, communications, HR, and external partners.
implementation_examples: Maintain a pre-defined incident response call tree and stakeholder contact list that is updated quarterly
Establish relationships with external incident response firms, law enforcement, and regulatory contacts before an incident occurs
Include legal counsel, public relations, and executive leadership in the incident response team as standing members

subcategories.name: Incident reports are communicated
subcategories.description: Incident information is communicated to relevant stakeholders in a timely manner, with appropriate detail and according to legal and regulatory obligations.
implementation_examples: Develop notification templates for different audiences including internal leadership, affected customers, regulators, and law enforcement
Establish a communication protocol that defines who is authorized to communicate about incidents and the approval process for external messaging
Track regulatory notification deadlines and automate reminders to ensure timely breach reporting obligations are met

categories.name: Incident Analysis
categories.description: Incidents are analyzed to determine their nature, scope, impact, and root cause to support effective response and future prevention.

subcategories.name: Incident notifications are received and processed
subcategories.description: Incoming incident notifications from automated systems, users, and external sources are received, triaged, and acted upon consistently.
implementation_examples: Establish a centralized incident intake process with a ticketing system that accepts reports via email, web form, phone, and API
Implement automated alert enrichment that appends asset context, user information, and threat intelligence to incoming notifications
Define triage SLAs for different notification types with critical alerts requiring initial assessment within 15 minutes

subcategories.name: Incidents are investigated
subcategories.description: Thorough investigations are conducted to determine the root cause, scope, and methods of each incident.
implementation_examples: Follow standardized investigation playbooks that outline data collection, analysis steps, and timeline reconstruction for common incident types
Preserve forensic evidence following chain of custody procedures for incidents that may lead to legal action or regulatory investigation
Use case management tools in the SIEM to document investigation findings, track evidence artifacts, and maintain an investigation timeline

subcategories.name: The impact of incidents is understood
subcategories.description: The operational, financial, legal, and reputational impact of incidents is assessed and documented to inform response prioritization and disclosure.
implementation_examples: Engage business unit representatives during incident analysis to assess operational impact on critical services and processes
Use a standardized impact assessment template that captures data types affected, systems compromised, and services disrupted
Quantify financial impact including response costs, business interruption losses, and potential regulatory fines for incident reporting

subcategories.name: Forensic evidence is collected, preserved, and analyzed
subcategories.description: Forensic data is collected and handled in a manner that preserves its integrity and admissibility for legal, regulatory, and internal purposes.
implementation_examples: Deploy forensic data collection tools that capture memory, disk, and network artifacts with cryptographic hash verification
Maintain a forensic lab environment for analyzing evidence without risking contamination or affecting production systems
Engage certified forensic examiners for incidents involving criminal activity, legal proceedings, or significant regulatory exposure

categories.name: Incident Mitigation
categories.description: Incident containment and eradication activities are performed to limit damage and remove threats from the environment.

subcategories.name: Incidents are contained
subcategories.description: Actions are taken to prevent the spread of an incident and limit its impact on organizational assets and operations.
implementation_examples: Implement network isolation procedures that can quickly disconnect affected systems or segments at the switch or firewall level
Maintain pre-approved containment playbooks with specific actions for ransomware, malware outbreak, insider threat, and account compromise scenarios
Deploy a security orchestration, automation, and response (SOAR) platform to automate initial containment actions such as user account suspension

subcategories.name: Incidents are eradicated
subcategories.description: Threats are removed from the environment through comprehensive remediation actions including malware removal, system rebuilding, and account cleanup.
implementation_examples: Develop eradication playbooks that specify the steps to remove malicious artifacts, revoke compromised credentials, and rebuild affected systems
Use endpoint detection and response tools to remotely remediate malware across multiple endpoints simultaneously
Conduct a thorough validation scan after eradication to confirm that all malicious artifacts have been removed and systems are clean

subcategories.name: Vulnerabilities exploited during incidents are identified and remediated
subcategories.description: The specific vulnerabilities that were exploited during an incident are identified and remediated to prevent recurrence.
implementation_examples: Document root cause vulnerabilities during post-incident analysis and create expedited remediation tasks in the vulnerability management system
Apply emergency patches or compensating controls for exploited vulnerabilities within 24 hours of confirmation
Conduct a targeted vulnerability scan across all similar systems to ensure the exploited vulnerability does not exist elsewhere in the environment

categories.name: Incident Improvements
categories.description: Lessons learned from incidents are captured and used to improve the organization's incident response capabilities and overall security posture.

subcategories.name: Incident response plans incorporate lessons learned
subcategories.description: Lessons learned from incident handling activities are documented and used to update incident response plans, playbooks, and procedures.
implementation_examples: Conduct formal lessons-learned meetings within two weeks of each major incident with representatives from all involved teams
Maintain a lessons-learned repository that tracks identified gaps, improvement actions, and their closure status
Update incident response playbooks within 30 days of incorporating lessons learned from an incident

subcategories.name: Incident response strategies are updated
subcategories.description: Overall incident response strategies, tools, and capabilities are enhanced based on insights gained from actual incidents and industry developments.
implementation_examples: Review and update the incident response technology stack annually based on capability gaps identified during incident handling
Incorporate new attack techniques and adversary tactics into incident response playbooks as they emerge in the threat landscape
Benchmark incident response capabilities against industry frameworks and peer organizations to identify strategic improvement opportunities

functions.name: Recover
functions.description: The organization restores assets and operations that were affected by cybersecurity incidents to a state of normalcy and learns from recovery activities.

categories.name: Recovery Plan Execution
categories.description: Recovery plans are executed to restore affected systems, data, and services in a timely and coordinated manner.

subcategories.name: The recovery plan is executed
subcategories.description: The recovery plan is activated and executed to restore systems, data, and services affected by an incident in accordance with defined priorities and timelines.
implementation_examples: Activate the disaster recovery plan and incident-specific recovery playbook once incident containment and eradication are confirmed
Prioritize recovery of critical services based on business impact analysis rankings and defined recovery time objectives
Document each recovery step with timestamps, responsible parties, and verification checkpoints to ensure completeness

subcategories.name: Recovery plan coordination with stakeholders is established and maintained
subcategories.description: Recovery activities are coordinated with internal and external stakeholders to ensure alignment and minimize confusion during restoration.
implementation_examples: Hold regular recovery coordination meetings with IT, business units, vendors, and third-party recovery service providers
Establish clear communication channels and decision-making authority for recovery phase activities and prioritization decisions
Coordinate with external partners and suppliers to ensure their recovery timelines align with organizational restoration needs

subcategories.name: Recovery plan communication is managed
subcategories.description: Timely and accurate information about recovery progress and expected timelines is communicated to affected stakeholders.
implementation_examples: Provide regular status updates to executive leadership, affected business units, and external stakeholders on recovery progress
Maintain a recovery status dashboard that shows real-time restoration progress for each critical service
Develop communication templates for different recovery phases to ensure consistent and accurate messaging

categories.name: Recovery Improvements
categories.description: Recovery plans and capabilities are improved based on lessons learned from recovery activities and exercises.

subcategories.name: Recovery plans incorporate lessons learned
subcategories.description: Insights gained from recovery activities and exercises are documented and integrated into recovery plans to improve future outcomes.
implementation_examples: Conduct recovery-focused lessons-learned sessions within two weeks of completing recovery operations from any significant incident
Document gaps in recovery plans, tools, and procedures and assign ownership for remediation actions with target completion dates
Update recovery playbooks to reflect lessons learned including new contact information, revised procedures, and improved restoration sequences

subcategories.name: Recovery strategies are updated
subcategories.description: The organization's overall recovery strategies, technologies, and capabilities are enhanced based on changes in the operating environment and emerging best practices.
implementation_examples: Review and update disaster recovery and business continuity strategies annually to address changes in technology architecture and business requirements
Evaluate and adopt new recovery technologies such as cloud-based disaster recovery, automated failover, and immutable backup solutions
Benchmark recovery capabilities against industry standards and incorporate improvements identified through exercises and actual recovery events

categories.name: Communications
categories.description: Communications during and after recovery are managed to restore stakeholder confidence and meet legal and regulatory obligations.

subcategories.name: Public relations and communications are managed
subcategories.description: External communications about recovery activities are coordinated to manage public perception and provide accurate, timely information.
implementation_examples: Coordinate with the public relations team to develop and manage external messaging about recovery progress and service restoration timelines
Establish a media monitoring process to track public sentiment and correct misinformation during the recovery phase
Prepare holding statements and frequently asked questions for common recovery scenarios to enable rapid response to media inquiries

subcategories.name: Reputation repair activities are performed
subcategories.description: Activities are undertaken to restore the organization's reputation and stakeholder trust following a cybersecurity incident.
implementation_examples: Develop a reputation management plan that includes customer outreach, transparency about what occurred, and actions taken to prevent recurrence
Engage with affected customers through personalized communications offering support, credit monitoring, or other remediation services
Publish a post-incident report detailing the incident, response actions, and improvements made to rebuild stakeholder confidence

subcategories.name: Coordination with external stakeholders is managed
subcategories.description: Communications with regulators, law enforcement, partners, and other external stakeholders are managed to meet obligations and maintain trust.
implementation_examples: Notify regulatory bodies in accordance with applicable breach notification laws and maintain documentation of all communications
Coordinate with law enforcement agencies as appropriate and provide necessary evidence and cooperation for criminal investigations
Communicate incident resolution and remediation steps to partners, suppliers, and other ecosystem stakeholders to rebuild trust
